experiment: Static-baseline feasibility check (SECOND_CONVERSATION.md, raised in team conversation)

checks.note: Present, ~45.3 MB. (Run `cd code && yarn storybook:ui:build` to (re)build.)

checks.description: Compare static-build index.json vs live Storybook
checks.note: Identical story sets — static is a faithful baseline.

conclusion: Static-baseline approach is mechanically feasible. A `storybook-static` folder served on a parallel port returns a valid index.json and serves iframe URLs identically to the live Storybook. Iteration-1.5 design: addon-before-after detects the folder, spins up a sidecar HTTP server, and uses its iframe URLs as the baseline. Open questions for the kickoff: (a) how does the addon machinery integrate with a sidecar process? (b) does this work on Webpack-builder Storybooks (test separately)? (c) how stale does the static build become during a long agent session — needs UX guidance for "rebuild baseline" CTA.
